name: 15_Image_Captioning_ShowAndTell_Flickr8k_DL
query: This is my current PyTorch project: Develop an automatic image captioning system using the Show and Tell model. Here I found a repo can guide you: https://github.com/sgrvinod/a-PyTorch-Tutorial-to-Image-Captioning. Use the dataset Flickr8k dataset, downloading it from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k) and load it in `src/data_loader.py`. The system should generate descriptions of sample images and save them to `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`. An attention mechanism must be implemented in `src/model.py`. Save the pre-trained model as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`. Visualize the attention weights and save it to `results/figures/attention_weights.png`. The dataset should load smoothly, with proper error handling if any issues arise. Ideally, the attention mechanism should clearly highlight the image regions that are most influential in generating captions.

requirements.criteria: The pre-trained "Show and Tell" model is used.
The "Flickr8k" dataset, potentially downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.
The attention mechanism is implemented in `src/model.py`.
Generated descriptions of sample images are saved in `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.
The trained model is saved as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.
A visualization of attention weights is saved as `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

preferences.criteria: The dataset should load smoothly, with clear error handling if any issues arise during download.
The attention mechanism should clearly highlight the image regions that contribute most to the generated captions.

judge_stats.criteria: The pre-trained "Show and Tell" model is used.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided code does not show use of a pre-trained Show and Tell model. In `src/model.py`, `build_model()` only returns `"model placeholder"` and contains no checkpoint loading logic such as `torch.load(...)` or instantiation of a Show-and-Tell architecture. Although `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt` exists in the workspace, there is no code or execution evidence connecting this file to model loading or inference. The trajectory evidence also explicitly states there is no execution trace confirming that a pre-trained Show and Tell model was loaded or used.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Flickr8k" dataset, potentially downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load the Flickr8k dataset. The file only contains placeholder logic: `load_dataset()` returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and there is no import or use of Hugging Face `datasets`, no file/path handling for Flickr8k images/captions, and no dataset download/read code. Mentioning `"Flickr8k"` in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`/`KEYWORDS` is not evidence of loading the dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: The attention mechanism is implemented in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement any attention mechanism. The file only defines `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, an empty `KEYWORDS` list, `requirement_notes()`, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"` (or `"ResNet-18 imported from PyTorch"` conditionally). There are no attention-related classes, layers, computations, or imports beyond `Path` and `torch`.

judge_stats.criteria: Generated descriptions of sample images are saved in `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt` exists, its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is not evidence of actual generated descriptions for sample images. The provided evidence also includes no trajectory or code/output showing a generation step that produced and saved image descriptions to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: The trained model is saved as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The workspace tree explicitly lists `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`, and the file content section confirms that “The file exists” at that exact path. This satisfies the requirement that the trained model is saved as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

judge_stats.criteria: A visualization of attention weights is saved as `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although a file exists at `results/figures/attention_weights.png`, its provided content is not a valid image; it contains an error message: `"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`. With no trajectory evidence confirming successful visualization generation, the requirement that an attention-weights visualization be saved as a valid PNG is not met.
